docs.title: Lumina Prompt Engine
docs.text: Multi-model prompt orchestration and evaluation — generate model-specific prompts from a Persona, rank them across six LLM engines, and score each with a Judge-LLM using five criteria.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
1. You define a Persona (identity, intent, output format).
  2. Six engines generate optimized prompts in parallel (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Llama, Groq).
  3. A DeepEval judge scores each prompt on five criteria and ranks results.
  4. Export PDF or Excel reports from the dashboard.

docs.title: Quick try
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/navendubrajesh/lumina_prompt_engine.git
cd lumina_prompt_engine
./setup.sh          # or .\setup.ps1 on Windows
# Add GROQ_API_KEY to .env for MONEYSAVER mode
uvicorn backend.main:app --reload --port 8000
cd frontend && npm run dev

Open http://localhost:3000 — enable MONEYSAVER mode to run all engines via Groq (free tier).

docs.title: Stack
docs.text: Layer Location Role Backend backend/ FastAPI, multi-engine router, DeepEval judge, pipeline Frontend frontend/ Next.js dashboard (Persona Studio, matrix, exports) CrewAI crewai/ Optional AMP deployment; same pipeline logic Config backend/app/config/engines.xml Engine definitions (real + MONEYSAVER)

docs.text:
• Quickstart — install, configure keys, run locally
  • Concepts — Persona, two-phase flow, MONEYSAVER
  • Architecture — pipeline, components, data flow
  • Engines — six LLM backends and XML config
  • Evaluation — five GEval criteria and scoring
  • API Reference — FastAPI endpoints and schemas
  • Frontend — Next.js UI and CrewAI client
  • CrewAI — AMP deployment and kickoff API
  • Deployment — Vercel + CrewAI production setup

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub repository
  • Product landing

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: FastAPI backend served by backend/main.py. Interactive docs at /docs when running locally.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: Environment URL Local http://localhost:8000 Production CrewAI AMP kickoff URL (see Deployment)

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Health check for load balancers and local dev.

Response:

{"status": "ok"}

docs.title: POST /generate-optimized-prompts
docs.text:
Generate prompts from a Persona; optionally skip evaluation.

Request body:

{
  "persona": {
    "identity": "Senior data scientist",
    "intent": "Analyze customer feedback",
    "output_format": "JSON with sentiment and themes"
  },
  "money_saver": true,
  "skip_evaluation": false
}
Field Type Default Description persona Persona required User context for generation money_saver bool false Route all engines + judge through Groq skip_evaluation bool false Return placeholders; use /evaluate-prompts next

Response: FinalResponse

{
  "results": [
    {
      "engine_output": {
        "engine_id": "openai_gpt4o",
        "display_name": "OpenAI GPT-4o",
        "prompt_text": "..."
      },
      "evaluation": {
        "contextual_alignment": {"score": 0.9, "reasoning": "..."},
        "instruction_clarity": {"score": 0.85, "reasoning": "..."},
        "constraint_adherence": {"score": 0.88, "reasoning": "..."},
        "robustness": {"score": 0.82, "reasoning": "..."},
        "efficiency": {"score": 0.91, "reasoning": "..."},
        "overall_score": 0.872
      }
    }
  ],
  "summary": "Ranked 6 prompts. Top: OpenAI GPT-4o (0.87).",
  "suggested_title": "Customer Feedback Analysis"
}

docs.title: POST /evaluate-prompts
docs.text:
Score existing prompts without regenerating.

Request body:

{
  "persona": { "identity": "...", "intent": "...", "output_format": "..." },
  "engine_outputs": [
    {
      "engine_id": "openai_gpt4o",
      "display_name": "OpenAI GPT-4o",
      "prompt_text": "..."
    }
  ],
  "money_saver": true
}

Response: Same FinalResponse shape with real evaluation scores.

docs.title: Schemas
docs.text:
Defined in backend/app/models.py:

Model Fields Persona identity, intent, output_format (all strings) PromptEngineOutput engine_id, display_name, prompt_text CriterionScore score, reasoning EvaluationMetrics five criteria + overall_score EvaluatedEngineOutput engine_output, evaluation FinalResponse results[], summary, suggested_title

docs.title: CORS
docs.text: FastAPI allows origins from CORS_ORIGINS env var (comma-separated). Default includes http://localhost:3000 for local Next.js.

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Status Cause 422 Invalid request body 500 No engine outputs (missing keys, all providers failed)

Check server logs for provider-specific LiteLLM errors.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: System design and data flow.

docs.title: High-level diagram
docs.text:
Persona (identity, intent, output_format)
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│ MultiEngineRouter │── parallel ──► 6 × PromptEngineOutput
└───────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│ DeepEval Judge    │── parallel ──► 6 × EvaluationMetrics
└───────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│ Title suggestion  │──► suggested_title (≤8 words)
└───────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
   FinalResponse → Frontend / PDF / Excel

docs.title: Repository layout
docs.text:
lumina_prompt_engine/
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py                 # FastAPI app
│   └── app/
│       ├── models.py           # Pydantic schemas
│       ├── pipeline.py         # Shared generate→evaluate→title
│       ├── title_suggestion.py
│       ├── engines/            # Router, providers, LiteLLM calls
│       ├── evaluator/          # DeepEval GEval judge
│       └── config/engines.xml
├── frontend/                   # Next.js  app/, components/, lib/
├── crewai/                     # CrewAI crew + Lumina pipeline tool
├── docs/                       # This documentation (MkDocs)
├── setup.ps1 / setup.sh
└── pyproject.toml

docs.title: Shared pipeline
docs.text:
backend/app/pipeline.py is the single source of truth for generation logic. Both FastAPI and CrewAI import run_pipeline_async so MONEYSAVER behaviour stays identical.

Key steps:

  1. _money_saver_env() — temporarily sets env vars so all providers use Groq
  2. MultiEngineRouter.generate_all() — parallel prompt generation
  3. evaluate_prompt() — parallel GEval scoring (skipped if skip_evaluation)
  4. suggest_run_title() — short title via LiteLLM (max_tokens=50)

docs.title: Engine router
docs.text:
MultiEngineRouter loads engine definitions from engines.xml:

  • Normal mode — real model IDs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.)
  • MONEYSAVER — ENGINES_MONEYSAVER list; same display names, all Groq

Generation uses Instructor + LiteLLM with a shared system prompt emphasizing clarity, constraints, and token efficiency.

docs.title: Frontend architecture
docs.text:
• Next.js App Router (frontend/app/)
  • API client (lib/api-client.ts) — two-phase FastAPI calls
  • CrewAI client (lib/crewai-client.ts) — kickoff + poll when AMP URL is configured
  • Exports — pdf-generator.ts, evaluation-matrix.tsx (xlsx)

docs.title: CrewAI integration
docs.text:
The crewai/ package wraps run_pipeline as a CrewAI tool. Deploy to AMP with project root = repo root so backend imports resolve.

See CrewAI and Deployment.

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text: Core ideas behind Lumina Prompt Engine.

docs.title: Persona
docs.text:
A Persona is the user input that drives prompt generation:

Field Meaning Example identity Who the user is Senior data scientist at a fintech startup intent What they want to achieve Extract structured insights from customer feedback output_format Desired structure JSON with keys: sentiment, themes, action_items

All engines receive the same Persona and produce model-specific optimized prompts tuned to each LLM's strengths.

docs.title: Two-phase UX
docs.text:
The frontend uses a two-phase flow for responsiveness:

sequenceDiagram
    participant UI as Dashboard
    participant API as FastAPI
    UI->>API: POST /generate-optimized-prompts (skip_evaluation=true)
    API-->>UI: Prompts + placeholder scores
    UI->>UI: Show prompts immediately
    UI->>API: POST /evaluate-prompts
    API-->>UI: Real GEval scores
    UI->>UI: Update matrix + ranking

Phase 1 returns prompts fast; Phase 2 runs the DeepEval judge in parallel. CrewAI mode can collapse both phases into one kickoff.

docs.title: MONEYSAVER mode
docs.text:
When money_saver=True:

  • All six engine cards keep the same display names (OpenAI GPT-4o, Claude, etc.)
  • Every engine call routes through Groq (groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile)
  • The judge also uses Groq (USE_GROQ_FOR_JUDGE=1)
  • Only GROQ_API_KEY is required

This lets you demo the full matrix on Groq's free tier without multi-provider billing.

docs.title: Evaluation and ranking
docs.text: Each prompt receives five scores (0.0–1.0) plus reasoning text. Overall score is the arithmetic mean of the five criteria. Results sort descending so the champion prompt is obvious.

docs.title: Exports
docs.text: PDF and Excel exports are client-side only — they consume the FinalResponse JSON already in the browser. No extra API calls.

docs.title: Deployment modes
docs.text:
Mode Backend Frontend Local dev FastAPI on :8000 Next.js on :3000 Production CrewAI AMP kickoff API Vercel (from frontend/)

When NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL is set, the frontend skips FastAPI and polls CrewAI directly.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Guidelines for extending Lumina Prompt Engine.

docs.title: Development setup
docs.text:
1. Fork and clone the repository
  2. Run ./setup.sh or .\setup.ps1
  3. Create a branch for your change
  4. Run backend and frontend locally (see Quickstart)

docs.title: Code areas
docs.text: Change type Location New engine backend/app/config/engines.xml, providers Judge criteria backend/app/evaluator/judge.py Pipeline logic backend/app/pipeline.py (keep FastAPI + CrewAI in sync) API routes backend/main.py UI frontend/components/, frontend/lib/ CrewAI crewai/src/lumina_crew/ Docs docs/ + mkdocs.yml

docs.title: Shared pipeline rule
docs.text: Never duplicate generate/evaluate logic in FastAPI and CrewAI separately. Always call run_pipeline_async from pipeline.py.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Update relevant docs/*.md when behaviour changes
  • Keep README.md and SETUP.md in sync with quickstart paths
  • Run MkDocs build locally:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs build --strict

docs.title: Pull requests
docs.text:
1. Describe the Persona/use case you tested
  2. Note which mode (MONEYSAVER vs full) and which keys you used
  3. Include screenshots for UI changes
  4. Ensure uvicorn backend.main:app and npm run build in frontend/ succeed

docs.title: Related documents
docs.text:
• CODE_PLAN_AGENTS_AND_TOOLS.md — original agent/tool design
  • SETUP.md — detailed setup troubleshooting

docs.title: License
docs.text: Follow the license in the repository root. Contributions are welcome via pull request.

docs.title: CrewAI
docs.text: Optional deployment path using CrewAI AMP (Agent Management Platform).

docs.title: Package layout
docs.text:
crewai/
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
└── src/lumina_crew/
    ├── main.py          # CLI entry: python -m lumina_crew.main
    ├── crew.py          # Crew definition
    └── tools/
        └── lumina_pipeline.py   # Wraps run_pipeline_async

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
The CrewAI tool calls the same run_pipeline_async from backend/app/pipeline.py:

  • Identical MONEYSAVER behaviour
  • Same six engines and DeepEval judge
  • Returns FinalResponse JSON to the crew

docs.title: Local test
docs.text:
From repo root:

cd crewai
PYTHONPATH=.. uv run python -m lumina_crew.main

Requires GROQ_API_KEY (or full provider keys) in root .env.

docs.title: AMP deployment
docs.text:
1. Create a CrewAI AMP project
  2. Set project root to the repository root (not crewai/ alone) so backend imports work
  3. Point entry to the lumina crew module
  4. Add secrets: GROQ_API_KEY, and other keys if not using MONEYSAVER
  5. Deploy and copy the kickoff URL + bearer token

docs.title: Frontend integration
docs.text:
Configure Vercel (or local .env.local):

NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL=https://your-deployment.kickoff.crewai.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BEARER_TOKEN=...

The dashboard then uses kickoff + polling instead of FastAPI.

docs.title: Kickoff payload
docs.text:
Typical kickoff body (exact shape depends on crew inputs):

{
  "inputs": {
    "identity": "...",
    "intent": "...",
    "output_format": "...",
    "money_saver": true
  }
}

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Issue Fix ModuleNotFoundError: backend Set AMP project root to repo root; PYTHONPATH includes parent Empty crew output Check AMP logs; verify API keys in AMP secrets Timeout on frontend Increase poll interval/timeout in crewai-client.ts

See crewai/README.md in the repo for AMP-specific notes.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: Production setup: Vercel (frontend) + CrewAI AMP (backend pipeline).

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
User browser
    │
    ▼
Vercel (Next.js frontend)
    │  NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL
    ▼
CrewAI AMP kickoff API
    │
    ▼
lumina_crew tool → backend/app/pipeline.py
    │
    ▼
6 engines + DeepEval judge → FinalResponse

Local development uses FastAPI on port 8000 instead of CrewAI.

docs.title: Step 1 — Deploy CrewAI backend
docs.text:
1. Push lumina_prompt_engine to GitHub
  2. In CrewAI AMP, create a project linked to the repo
  3. Project root: repository root (required for backend imports)
  4. Add secrets to AMP:
  5. GROQ_API_KEY (minimum for MONEYSAVER)
  6. Or full provider keys for non–MONEYSAVER runs
  7. Deploy and note:
  8. Kickoff URL (e.g. https://xxx.kickoff.crewai.com)
  9. Bearer token

Test with curl or the CrewAI dashboard before wiring the frontend.

docs.title: Step 2 — Deploy frontend to Vercel
docs.text:
1. Import the GitHub repo in Vercel
  2. Root Directory: frontend
  3. Framework preset: Next.js
  4. Environment variables:
Variable Value NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL AMP kickoff base URL NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BEARER_TOKEN AMP bearer token
  1. Deploy — your dashboard URL is live (e.g. https://lumina-xxx.vercel.app)

Do not set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in production unless you self-host FastAPI.

docs.title: Step 3 — Documentation site
docs.text:
Full MkDocs documentation is published to:

https://navendubrajesh.github.io/lumina_prompt_engine/

Built from this repo's docs/ via the portfolio sync workflow.

Product landing (single-page overview + doc embed):

https://navendubrajesh.github.io/lumina_prompt_engine.enterprise/

docs.title: Self-hosted FastAPI (optional)
docs.text:
Run behind your own reverse proxy:

uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

Set on the frontend:

NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api.yourdomain.com

Ensure CORS_ORIGINS includes your frontend origin.

docs.title: Environment checklist
docs.text: Variable Where Required GROQ_API_KEY AMP / .env MONEYSAVER Provider keys AMP / .env Full mode NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL Vercel Production UI NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BEARER_TOKEN Vercel Production UI CORS_ORIGINS FastAPI host Self-hosted only

docs.title: vercel.json
docs.text: Root vercel.json may redirect or configure monorepo behaviour — prefer setting Root Directory = frontend in the Vercel UI for clarity.

docs.title: Engines
docs.text: Lumina routes prompt generation through six LLM backends defined in backend/app/config/engines.xml.

docs.title: Engine matrix
docs.text:
Display name Provider Normal model MONEYSAVER model OpenAI GPT-4o OpenAI gpt-4o groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile Claude 3.5 Sonnet Anthropic claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 Groq Llama 3.3 70B Gemini 1.5 Pro Google gemini-1.5-pro Groq Llama 3.3 70B Mistral Large Mistral mistral-large-latest Groq Llama 3.3 70B Llama 3.1 70B Together meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct-Turbo Groq Llama 3.3 70B Groq Llama 3.3 70B Groq groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile (unchanged)

MONEYSAVER keeps display names identical so the dashboard matrix looks the same while only GROQ_API_KEY is billed.

docs.title: Configuration file
docs.text:
backend/app/config/engines.xml defines each engine:

  • id — internal key (e.g. openai_gpt4o)
  • display_name — shown in UI and exports
  • provider — LiteLLM provider string
  • model — model ID for normal mode
  • money_saver_model — optional override when MONEYSAVER is on

docs.title: Generation stack
docs.text:
Each engine call uses:

  • Instructor — structured output into PromptEngineOutput
  • LiteLLM — unified provider interface
  • Shared system prompt — SYSTEM_PROMPT in engines/base.py (clarity, constraints, efficiency)

docs.title: API keys by provider
docs.text:
Provider Environment variable OpenAI OPENAI_API_KEY Anthropic ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Google GOOGLE_API_KEY Mistral MISTRAL_API_KEY Together (Llama) TOGETHER_API_KEY Groq GROQ_API_KEY

In MONEYSAVER mode, pipeline._money_saver_env copies GROQ_API_KEY into OpenAI/Google slots and sets USE_GROQ_FOR_JUDGE=1.

docs.title: Parallel execution
docs.text: MultiEngineRouter.generate_all() runs all configured engines concurrently with asyncio.gather. Failed engines log warnings; if zero engines succeed, the pipeline raises RuntimeError.

docs.title: Extending engines
docs.text:
1. Add an <engine> block to engines.xml
  2. Ensure the provider is supported by LiteLLM
  3. Add the corresponding API key to .env and backend/.env.example
  4. Update frontend types if display metadata changes

See also CODE_PLAN_AGENTS_AND_TOOLS.md for the original design rationale.

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text: Lumina scores each generated prompt with a Judge-LLM using DeepEval GEval metrics.

docs.title: Five criteria
docs.text:
Criterion What it measures Contextual alignment Does the prompt reflect the Persona's identity, intent, and output format? Instruction clarity Are instructions unambiguous and actionable? Constraint adherence Does the prompt enforce stated format and boundaries? Robustness Would the prompt handle edge cases and ambiguous inputs? Efficiency Is the prompt concise without sacrificing necessary detail?

Each criterion returns:

  • score — float from 0.0 to 1.0
  • reasoning — short explanation from the judge

docs.title: Overall score
docs.text:
overall_score = mean(contextual_alignment, instruction_clarity,
                     constraint_adherence, robustness, efficiency)

Results sort by overall_score descending in the API response and dashboard.

docs.title: Judge implementation
docs.text:
Location: backend/app/evaluator/judge.py

  • Uses GEval with LLMTestCase built from Persona JSON + generated prompt
  • Runs five metrics in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor (max 5 workers)
  • Default judge model: OpenAI (via DeepEval defaults)

docs.title: MONEYSAVER judge
docs.text:
When USE_GROQ_FOR_JUDGE=1:

LiteLLMModel(model="groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile", api_key=GROQ_API_KEY)

Set automatically by _money_saver_env() in the pipeline.

docs.title: Placeholder evaluation
docs.text: During Phase 1 (skip_evaluation=True), the API returns placeholder scores (zeros + "Evaluating…" reasoning) so the UI can render prompts immediately. Phase 2 replaces them with real GEval results.

docs.title: Evaluation-only endpoint
docs.text: POST /evaluate-prompts accepts a Persona plus existing PromptEngineOutput list — useful when the frontend already has prompts and only needs scoring.

docs.title: Tuning
docs.text:
GEval criteria strings live in judge.py. Adjust rubric text there to change judge behaviour. For production, consider:

  • Fixed judge model for reproducibility
  • Caching scores by prompt hash
  • Human-in-the-loop review for high-stakes use cases

See Evaluation criteria details in the code plan for original metric definitions.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text: Next.js dashboard in frontend/ — Persona Studio, evaluation matrix, and exports.

docs.title: Tech stack
docs.text:
• Next.js (App Router)
  • TypeScript
  • Tailwind CSS
  • jspdf + xlsx for client-side exports

docs.title: Key routes
docs.text: Path Component Purpose / app/page.tsx Main dashboard — Persona form + results Layout app/layout.tsx Theme, fonts, global styles

docs.title: Components
docs.text: Component Role persona-studio.tsx Identity, intent, output format inputs + MONEYSAVER toggle evaluation-matrix.tsx Ranked results table, score breakdown, export buttons loading-state.tsx Progress during generation/evaluation

docs.title: FastAPI (local / self-hosted)
docs.text:
lib/api-client.ts:

  1. generateOptimizedPrompts(persona, moneySaver, skipEvaluation=true) → Phase 1
  2. evaluatePrompts(persona, outputs, moneySaver) → Phase 2

Base URL from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (default http://localhost:8000).

docs.title: CrewAI (production)
docs.text:
When NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL is set, lib/crewai-client.ts:

  1. POST {base}/kickoff with Persona + money_saver
  2. Poll status until complete
  3. Parse FinalResponse from crew output

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Create frontend/.env.local:

# Local development
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000

# Production (Vercel) — CrewAI AMP
NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL=https://your-crew.kickoff.crewai.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BEARER_TOKEN=your_token

If NEXT_PUBLIC_CREWAI_BASE_URL is set, the FastAPI client is not used.

docs.title: Running
docs.text:
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev      # http://localhost:3000
npm run build    # production build
npm run start    # serve production build

docs.title: Vercel deployment
docs.text:
Root directory: frontend

Set environment variables in the Vercel dashboard. See Deployment.

docs.title: Theming
docs.text: CSS variables in app/globals.css support light/dark themes consistent with the portfolio site.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get Lumina Prompt Engine running locally in under ten minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • Node.js 18+
  • npm

docs.title: Automated setup
docs.text:
Windows (PowerShell):

.\setup.ps1

macOS / Linux:

chmod +x setup.sh && ./setup.sh

The script creates .venv, installs backend and frontend dependencies, and copies .env from backend/.env.example.

docs.title: Configure API keys
docs.text:
Edit .env in the project root:

# Minimum for MONEYSAVER mode (all engines + judge via Groq)
GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_...

# Full mode — one key per provider
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
GOOGLE_API_KEY=...
MISTRAL_API_KEY=...
TOGETHER_API_KEY=...
Mode Keys required MONEYSAVER GROQ_API_KEY only Full Keys for each engine you want to run; OPENAI_API_KEY minimum for judge

docs.title: Run locally
docs.text:
Terminal 1 — Backend:

.venv\Scripts\activate    # Windows
# source .venv/bin/activate  # macOS/Linux
uvicorn backend.main:app --reload --port 8000

Terminal 2 — Frontend:

cd frontend && npm run dev
Service URL Dashboard http://localhost:3000 API docs (Swagger) http://localhost:8000/docs Health check http://localhost:8000/health

docs.title: First run
docs.text:
1. Open http://localhost:3000
  2. Fill in Identity, Intent, and Output format
  3. Enable MONEYSAVER mode if you only have a Groq key
  4. Submit — prompts appear first, then evaluation scores
  5. Download PDF or Excel from the results matrix

docs.title: CrewAI local test
docs.text:
From repo root with GROQ_API_KEY in .env:

cd crewai
PYTHONPATH=.. uv run python -m lumina_crew.main

See CrewAI for AMP deployment.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: See the root SETUP.md for detailed troubleshooting (ports, imports, CORS, API keys).
